commit.message:
fix: minor code clarity cleanup (idx_tool rename, _type comment, KeyboardInterrupt comment)

- Rename idx_tool → idx in parser.py _build_active_summary
- Update _type alias comment in models.py to reflect current reality
- Add clarifying comment to bare except KeyboardInterrupt in cli.py

Closes #455

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address review comments

Move _validate_since_until() before _print_version_header() in both
summary() and cost() commands so that reversed --since/--until ranges
produce clean Click UsageError output without the version header.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address review comments

- Extract _validate_since_until helper to deduplicate reversed-range
  validation between summary and cost commands
- Use isoformat() instead of .date() in UsageError messages to preserve
  time component
- Fix test docstrings to match actual assertions (non-zero vs code 2)
- Update section headers and class names in test_report.py to reflect
  current behavior (no warning emitted for reversed ranges)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add browser and stop guidance to syu app (#144)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 12 platform-specific IPC examples (#64)

* Add file-watcher example with inotify-backed server and tests

Linux directory watcher using inotify that demonstrates IPC notifications.
Server watches directories and broadcasts FileChanged events to clients.
Seven integration tests cover watch/unwatch lifecycle, event notifications
for file creation/modification/deletion, and error handling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add sysmon example with /proc-based CPU, memory, and load monitoring

Server reads /proc/stat, /proc/meminfo, and /proc/loadavg to implement
GetCpuUsage, GetMemoryInfo, and GetLoadAverage RPCs. A monitoring thread
checks thresholds every second and fires HighCpuAlert/LowMemoryAlert
notifications.

Client queries all three methods in a formatted table and listens for
threshold notifications. Test suite covers all RPCs, both notification
types, invalid method handling, and RunLoop dispatch mode (8 tests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add disk-usage example with simulated volumes

Implement the disk-usage example under examples/macos/disk-usage/:

- disk_device.cpp: Simulated server with 3 volumes (/, /home, /tmp).
  Usage on / grows 1 GB every 3s; fires DiskSpaceLow when > 90%.
- disk_client.cpp: Interactive CLI with list, check <mount>, quit
  commands and bar-chart display. Prints notification alerts.
- disk_test.cpp: 8 Google Test cases covering GetVolumeCount,
  GetVolumeAt (valid/invalid), GetDiskUsage (known/unknown mount),
  DiskSpaceLow notification, UsedPercent consistency, RunLoop dispatch.
- CMakeLists.txt: Builds device, client, and test executables.
- README.md: IDL overview, build/run instructions, sample output.

All volumes are simulated — no platform-specific APIs — so the example
compiles and runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add audio-dsp example: simulated DSP effects processor

Implement server (dsp_device), client CLI (dsp_dashboard), and 12 Google
Test cases covering slot management, parameter updates, effect counting,
signal levels, invalid-slot errors, and ClipDetected/LevelUpdate
notifications.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add can-bus-ecu example: simulated ECU with OBD-II PIDs

Implement ecu_device (server with state machine simulation),
ecu_dashboard (interactive CLI client), and ecu_test (11 Google
Test cases covering all RPC methods, notifications, and RunLoop
dispatch). Cross-platform — compiles on Linux and Windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add BLE Heart Rate Monitor peripheral example

Implement simulated BLE HRM with GATT service model:
- ble_device: server with Heart Rate (0x180D) and Battery (0x180F)
  services, simulation thread for HR/battery updates
- ble_dashboard: interactive CLI client with service discovery,
  characteristic read, and notification subscription
- ble_test: 9 Google Test cases covering service count, get/read/
  write characteristics, notification enable/disable, error cases

All code is cross-platform (no macOS-specific APIs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add motor-controller example with simulated stepper motor

Implements MotorController IPC service with trapezoidal motion profiles,
limit switch detection, stall monitoring, and homing sequence. Includes:

- motor_device: simulated stepper motor server (10ms tick loop)
- motor_dashboard: interactive CLI client with status display
- motor_test: 10 Google Test cases covering all methods and notifications

All code is cross-platform (no Windows-specific APIs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CMake wiring and build warnings for platform examples

- Consolidate platform subdirectory includes in examples/CMakeLists.txt
- Guard Linux-only examples (sysmon, file-watcher) inside linux/CMakeLists.txt
- Remove duplicate add_subdirectory entries from parallel agent commits
- Fix -Werror=unused-result warnings in filewatcher_test.cpp

* Address code review findings across platform examples

- Fix dead code and wrong formula in battery_device timeToEmpty calc
- Move notifyXxx calls outside mutex locks in motor, gpio, procmon, svcstat
- Add can-bus-ecu to windows/CMakeLists.txt (was missing)
- Replace fragile reference capture in ble_device with member m_simRunning
- Add explicit null termination after strncpy in disk_device
- Move ecu_device printf inside lock scope to avoid data race

* Fix motor simulation crossover on coarse-timer platforms

On Windows (~15ms timer resolution) and macOS, a single simulation
tick can overshoot the MoveTo target position. Add post-update
crossover detection in both the production device and test device
to snap to target when position crosses or reaches the destination.

Fixes CI failures: MoveTo_ReachesTarget, MoveWithoutHome_Allowed

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cli): raise click.UsageError for reversed --since/--until range (#454)

Move reversed date-range validation from _filter_sessions (report layer)
to the summary and cost CLI commands. This replaces the raw Python
UserWarning with a proper click.UsageError that exits with code 2 and
a human-readable message.

- cli.py: Add since > until check in summary() and cost() before
  calling render functions
- report.py: Remove warnings.warn from _filter_sessions; silently
  return [] for reversed ranges
- test_cli.py: Add CliRunner tests for both summary and cost with
  reversed range
- test_report.py: Update existing tests to assert no warning is emitted
- test_e2e.py: Update e2e test to expect non-zero exit instead of
  UserWarning

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: build complete MarTech Stack Auditor application

- Add 200+ MarTech tools database (src/data/martech-tools.ts) with overlap/gap detection and audit scoring logic
- Add Zod validation schema (src/lib/schemas.ts) for audit API requests
- Add POST /api/audit route with Resend email report integration
- Add interactive StackAuditor client component with category tabs, tool selection, animated results
- Update layout.tsx with SEO metadata and system fonts
- Add .env.example for RESEND_API_KEY configuration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: DarlingMarketingandTech <268082351+DarlingMarketingandTech@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: add weekly scheduled run for dependency drift detection (#126)

Add a schedule trigger to ci.yml (Mondays 06:00 UTC) so cargo audit
and npm audit run automatically between releases, catching new CVEs
before the next PR. cancel-in-progress is disabled for scheduled runs
so alerts are never silently dropped.

Document the weekly audit cadence in CONTRIBUTING.md so contributors
know they do not need to run manual audits.

Closes #116

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add URL deep linking, keyboard search navigation, and favicon to syu app (#132)

- Hash-based URL routing: navigation state persisted in window.location.hash
  (#section/item-id format). On load, restores state from hash. jumpToItem
  and selectSection update the hash via history.replaceState so items are
  directly shareable via URL.

- Keyboard navigation in search: ArrowDown/Up moves through results,
  Enter selects focused or sole result, Escape clears query. Focused
  result highlighted with ring-2 ring-sky-400. Added role=combobox,
  aria-expanded, aria-controls ARIA attributes.

- Item chip label: 'Items in this document' label added above pill row.
  Each pill button now has title attribute showing the full item title.

- Favicon: add app/public/favicon.svg and link it in index.html.

Closes #122

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: fix mobile/tablet responsive layout and accessibility in syu app (#131)

- Convert header nav to horizontally scrollable on small screens
  to prevent wrapping and reclaim vertical space on 375px viewports
- Lower two-column grid breakpoint from lg (1024px) to md (768px)
  so tablet users see content immediately without scrolling sidebar
- Add resetNavigation() and make 'syu' logo a home/reset button
- Truncate workspace path to last 2 components with … prefix
- Add role/aria-label/aria-valuenow/min/max to progress bars in
  LayerNavigationCard for screen-reader accessibility
- Update Playwright test to match new button role for logo

Closes #123

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add onboarding banner, trace tooltips, and back navigation to syu app (#130)

* feat: add onboarding banner, trace tooltips, and back navigation to syu app

Add three UX improvements for first-time users:

1. Dismissible welcome banner at the top of the main content area
   explaining the four-layer model. Dismissal is persisted in
   localStorage so the banner only shows once per browser.

2. Trace panel tooltips: SYMBOLS and DOC CONTAINS now have ⓘ title
   attributes explaining what each field verifies. Empty symbols
   display as 'any symbol (wildcard)'; empty doc_contains displays
   as 'not declared' instead of bare '—'.

3. Navigation history back button: jumpToItem() pushes the previous
   item to a history stack (capped at 5). A '← Back' button appears
   in the detail header when history is available.

Closes #121

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* app: address onboarding review feedback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

meaning, a minimal YAML example that triggers it, and a step-by-step

refactor: split build_session_summary into focused helpers (#224)

Extract the ~200-line build_session_summary function into 4 focused
private helpers:

- _first_pass(): single pass over events to extract identity, shutdown
  data, and counters
- _detect_resume(): scan post-shutdown events for resume indicators
- _build_completed_summary(): merge shutdown cycles into SessionSummary
- _build_active_summary(): model inference + config fallback for active
  sessions

Two frozen dataclasses (_FirstPassResult, _ResumeInfo) carry state
between phases. The public API (signature + return type) is unchanged.

Closes #224

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve CI failure — add test for negative shutdown_model_calls guard

The diff-cover check failed because line 597 of report.py (the
defensive clamp of shutdown_model_calls to 0 when active_model_calls
exceeds model_calls) was not covered by tests.

Added test_negative_shutdown_model_calls_clamped_to_zero to cover
this edge case, bringing diff coverage to 100%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address review comments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: show shutdown-only model calls on per-model cost rows (#409)

In render_cost_view, the per-model row displayed s.model_calls (total
across all periods) while all other columns used shutdown-only data from
s.model_metrics. For resumed sessions this caused the visual sum of
per-model + ↳ rows to exceed the grand total.

Subtract s.active_model_calls so the per-model row is consistent with
the other shutdown-only columns. For non-resumed sessions
active_model_calls is 0, so behaviour is unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: enable doctest execution for format_tokens/format_duration (#352)

Restore doctest examples in _formatting.py and enable --doctest-modules
in pyproject.toml so pytest collects and verifies them. Added boundary
test (format_duration(60000) -> '1m'). 9 doctest examples now run as
part of the normal test suite.

Closes #352

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: exclude developer docs from wheel distribution (#330)

Add exclude directive to hatchling wheel config to prevent shipping
src/copilot_usage/docs/ (plan.md, architecture.md, implementation.md,
changelog.md) in the wheel. Add packaging test to verify.

Closes #330

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: wrap bare loguru import in TYPE_CHECKING guard (#291)

Move the bare 'import loguru' (kept only for pyright to resolve the
string annotation 'loguru.Record') behind TYPE_CHECKING. Removes the
runtime import and the noqa: F401 suppression.

Items 1 and 3 from #291 were already fixed in prior commits:
- toolRequests: already uses lambda: [] (pyright rejects default_factory=list)
- click/rich: already have version bounds (click>=8.1,<9, rich>=13,<15)

Closes #291

fix: replace assert with ValueError and deduplicate session sort key (#446)

Cleanup 1: Replace runtime assert in _render_summary_header with an
explicit ValueError raise so the guard works under python -O. Remove
the now-unnecessary # noqa: S101 / # nosec B101 suppressions.

Cleanup 2: Extract the duplicated session sort-key pattern into a
public session_sort_key() function in models.py. Replace the inner
_sort_key function in parser.py and the lambda in report.py with
calls to the shared helper.

Tests:
- _render_summary_header([]) raises ValueError
- Both callers guard against empty sessions before calling it
- session_sort_key is importable and returns expected values

Remove from report.py:

docs: explain symbols and doc_contains fields in getting-started guide (#100) (#105)

Step 3 previously showed doc_contains and symbols in YAML but never

docs: add key concepts terminology to configuration guide (#106)

* docs: add key concepts terminology section to configuration guide (#101)

Add a 'Key concepts' section near the top of configuration.md defining
the terms used by the validation flags:
- orphaned item: spec item with no links to adjacent layers
- reciprocal link: two-way bidirectional confirmation of a relationship
- symbol trace: declaration naming the source functions that implement/test an item
- symbol trace coverage: enforcement that all public symbols are claimed

ci: run Playwright E2E tests in browser-app CI job (#97) (#113)

Add steps to the browser-app CI job to install Chromium via Playwright
and execute 'npm run test:e2e' so browser regressions are caught on
every PR.

The webServer in playwright.config.ts starts 'cargo run -- app .' using
the debug build already compiled in the same job, so no extra build step
is needed.

feat: add jump-to-item button in validation issue detail in syu app (#79) (#112)

When a validation issue's subject is a known spec item ID, show a
'→ View <ID>' button in the IssueDetail panel that calls jumpToItem()
to navigate directly to that spec item.

This creates a tight feedback loop: see a validation problem → click →
inspect the problematic spec item → fix it.

When a planned requirement has no tests or a planned feature has no

add and how to promote the item to 'implemented'.

feat: show validation error count badges on section navigation tabs (#92)

Closes #80

- Replace per-selected-section sectionIssueCount/sectionIssueHasError
  with sectionIssueSummaries: a Map<SectionKind, {count, hasError}>
  computed over all sections at once
- Each section nav button now renders a count badge when its section
  has validation issues: rose badge for errors, amber badge for warnings
- Badge includes an aria-label for accessibility
- sectionIssueCount and sectionIssueHasError are derived from the new
  map, so the sidebar 'issues' metric is unchanged

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add prerequisites section and validation error guide to getting-started (#89)

Closes #76

- Add 'Before you begin' section explaining install via release script or

fix: track detail view session by ID instead of positional index (#441)

Replace detail_idx (1-based positional index) with detail_session_id
(session ID string) in _interactive_loop. After auto-refresh rebuilds
the sessions list, the detail view now looks up the session by ID
rather than by position. This prevents showing the wrong session when
new sessions are prepended or existing sessions are removed.

deleted), the view falls back to the home screen.

feat: validate runtime command values in syu.yaml at config load time (#88)

Reject runtimes.python.command and runtimes.node.command values that
look like shell expressions.  The validator rejects any value that:
- Contains shell metacharacters (; | & $ ` ! () {} <> ' " \)
- Contains whitespace (spaces or newlines)
- Is an absolute path that does not point to an existing file

'auto' (and empty string) is always accepted.  Bare executable names
such as 'python3', 'node', 'bun' pass through unchanged; PATH search
happens later in resolve_runtime_command.

The error is surfaced with context pointing to the syu.yaml path so
users can identify and fix the offending value immediately.

Closes #68

commit.message:
fix: instruct implementer to include Closes keyword in PR body (#435)

The issue-implementer agent inconsistently included 'Closes #NNN' in PR
bodies because there was no explicit instruction to do so. This caused
issues to remain open after their fix PRs merged (e.g. #407, #420, #430).

Added explicit instruction to always include 'Closes #<issue_number>' in
the PR body so GitHub auto-closes the issue on merge.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add three new tests to document and lock in existing behavior:

- test_session_prefix_collision_returns_newest_by_mtime: verifies the
  session command's 'first discovered wins' contract when two sessions
  share a common prefix (newest by mtime is returned silently).

- TestNormalizeUntilNonUtcTimezone: two tests verifying that
  _normalize_until correctly preserves non-UTC timezone offsets when
  expanding midnight datetimes, and leaves non-midnight non-UTC
  datetimes unchanged.

commit.message:
Fix vHealth snapshot collection error - SnapshotTree has no config attribute

Fixed critical error in _collect_snapshot_files() method:
  Error: 'vim.vm.SnapshotTree' object has no attribute 'config'

Root cause:
  The code was trying to access snapshot.config on SnapshotTree objects,
  but SnapshotTree doesn't have a config attribute. It has properties like
  name, state, createTime, but not config.

Solution:
  Removed incorrect attempts to access snapshot.config and
  snapshot.config.hardware. These don't exist on SnapshotTree objects.

  Snapshot disk files are tracked through VM's disk layout, not through
  separate snapshot-specific files. The snapshot structure is maintained
  by vCenter and doesn't create separate orphaned files for snapshots.

Why this fix is correct:
  1. SnapshotTree is just metadata about snapshots (name, state, time)
  2. Actual snapshot disks (.vmdk files) are referenced by VM's disk array
  3. VM's vm.Layout.Disk already includes snapshot disks
  4. No need to separately crawl snapshots for file discovery
  5. This simplifies the code and removes the error

Result:
  - Eliminates repeated error messages
  - Cleaner zombie detection (only orphaned VM/template files)
  - No change in functionality (snapshot files already detected via VM disks)
  - All 79 tests passing

Error messages eliminated:
  [BEFORE] Error collecting snapshot files: 'vim.vm.SnapshotTree' object has no attribute 'config'
  [AFTER]  (no error - snapshots handled correctly)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add snapshot collection logging and mock snapshot support

Added debug logging to vSnapshot collector to help troubleshoot snapshot collection:
- Logs total VM count found
- Logs VM name, power state, and snapshot count for each VM
- Logs total snapshots collected at end
- Logs any errors during snapshot collection

Updated mock VM to support snapshot attribute (None by default) for testing.

This enables tracking why powered-off VM snapshots might not be appearing.

Debug output examples:
  [vSnapshot] Found 5 VMs total
  [vSnapshot] VM prod-server (poweredOn): 2 snapshots
  [vSnapshot] VM backup-vm (poweredOff): 1 snapshots
  [vSnapshot] VM empty-vm (poweredOff): no snapshots
  [vSnapshot] Total snapshots collected: 3

All 79 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add zombie vmtx template detection, age column, and snapshot age calculation

Added three major enhancements:

1. ZOMBIE VMTX (TEMPLATE) DETECTION:
   - Now detects and reports orphaned template files (.vmtx)
   - Shows message: 'Possibly a Zombie template! Please check.'
   - Includes same metadata as vmdk zombies (size, modified, age)
   - Properly skips reporting vmdk files if associated .vmtx exists

2. AGE COLUMN FOR ZOMBIE WARNINGS:
   - Added 'age' field to zombie warning records
   - Stores age in days calculated from file modification date
   - Used in Excel vHealth sheet to show zombie disk age
   - Helps prioritize storage cleanup by age

3. SNAPSHOT AGE CALCULATION:
   - Added 'age' field to vSnapshot records
   - Calculates snapshot age in days from creation time
   - Stored as integer (days)
   - Useful for identifying old snapshots to delete

Example zombie warnings:
  - Zombie vmdk: 'Possibly a Zombie vmdk file! Please check. (size: 5120.0 MB, modified: 2026-03-27T10:00:00, age: 5 days)'
  - Zombie template: 'Possibly a Zombie template! Please check. (size: 1024.0 MB, modified: 2026-02-01T10:00:00, age: 54 days)'

Snapshot records now include:
  - age: 5 (days old from creation)

All 79 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

3. Fix stale claim in implementation.md that pricing multipliers are
   'not used in any runtime calculations' — they are used by
   _estimate_premium_cost() for ~N estimates on active sessions.

4. Item 4 (narrow except Exception in _start_observer) was already

commit.message:
Enhance zombie debug logging with comprehensive properties

Expanded debug output to include ALL zombie disk properties:
- File size (both bytes and MB format)
- Modification date (ISO format)
- File age in days (calculated from modification)
- Inactive VM detection (name if orphaned from powered-off VM)
- Classification (permanently orphaned vs from inactive VM)

Debug format changed from:
  [ZOMBIE DEBUG] File NOT in registered: path size: bytes modified: date

To comprehensive format:
  [ZOMBIE DEBUG] File NOT in registered: path | size: bytes (MB) | modified: date | age: days | belongs to: vm-name

Examples:
  [ZOMBIE DEBUG] File NOT in registered: vm-folder/disk.vmdk | size: 5242880000 bytes (5120.0 MB) | modified: 2026-03-27T10:00:00.123456 | age: 5 days | belongs to inactive VM: old-vm

  [ZOMBIE DEBUG] File NOT in registered: orphan.vmdk | size: 1048576000 bytes (1000.0 MB) | modified: 2026-01-15T14:30:00 | age: 71 days | belongs to: permanently orphaned (no inactive VM match)

All 79 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address review comments

- Add minimal model_metrics dict to test_since_last_shutdown_row_with_model_none
  so has_shutdown_metrics=True reflects a realistic resumed session state
- Rephrase implementation.md to avoid equating 'resumed' with has_shutdown_metrics=True

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add enhanced zombie disk properties and inactive VM detection

Added comprehensive properties to zombie disk warnings:
- File size in MB (already present, improved formatting)
- Modification date with ISO format
- File age calculation in days
- Detection of whether disk belongs to inactive (powered-off) VM
- VM name when disk belongs to inactive VM

New method _build_inactive_vm_disk_map():
- Maps disk file paths to inactive VM metadata
- Tracks VM name, power state, and config path
- Collects from both vm.Layout.Disk (runtime) and config disks

Enhanced zombie warnings now include:
  'belongs to inactive VM: vm-name' when applicable

Debug logging enhanced with file age and inactive VM info

Example output:
  Possibly a Zombie vmdk file! Please check. (size: 5120.0 MB, modified: 2026-03-27T10:00:00, belongs to inactive VM: old-vm)

All 79 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: suppress misleading 'Since last shutdown' row for pure-active sessions (#419)

render_cost_view showed the '↳ Since last shutdown' sub-row for every
is_active session, including ones that were never shut down. This
produced duplicate stats under a misleading heading.

Change the condition from 'if s.is_active' to
'if s.is_active and s.has_shutdown_metrics' so the row only appears for
resumed sessions that have actual shutdown-baseline data.

- Update existing tests to use has_shutdown_metrics=True where they
  test shutdown-row rendering behavior
- Add TestRenderCostViewPureActiveNoShutdownRow with explicit tests for
  pure-active (no row) and resumed (row present) scenarios
- Update implementation.md to match the new condition

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address review comments

Treat max_events <= 0 as 'show nothing' in _render_recent_events to
avoid the Python slice quirk where events[-0:] returns all events.
Updated docstring and test to match the new semantics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: cover parser and render_detail edge cases (#418)

Add 11 tests for five coverage gaps identified in issue #418:

Gap 1 — malformed session.start (ValidationError):
- test_malformed_session_start_skipped: empty data → session_id='', start_time=None
- test_second_session_start_ignored_after_valid: first valid session.start wins

Gap 2 — active session, no model, has output tokens:
- test_active_no_model_output_tokens_preserved: model=None → model_metrics={}, tokens set

Gap 3 — multiple tool.execution_complete (first model wins):
- test_first_tool_model_wins: break on first non-None model
- test_tool_model_none_falls_through_to_second: None skipped, second used

Gap 4 — _format_relative_time negative delta:
- test_negative_delta_clamped_to_zero: -90s → '+0:00'
- test_negative_fractional_clamped_to_zero: -0.5s → '+0:00'
- test_zero_delta: 0s → '+0:00'
- test_event_before_session_start_renders_zero: integration test

Gap 5 — _render_recent_events max_events boundary:
- test_max_events_zero_shows_all: events[-0:] == events[:] (all shown)
- test_max_events_one_shows_last: only last event rendered

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: cover interactive loop and session command coverage gaps (#417)

Add 5 tests for untested branches in cli.py:

Gap 1 — Session index boundaries:
- test_interactive_session_index_zero: index 0 prints error
- test_interactive_session_index_negative: index -1 prints error

Gap 2 — Auto-refresh with detail_idx=None:
- test_auto_refresh_detail_idx_none: after returning from detail view,
  auto-refresh renders home (not detail), verifying the guard

Gap 3 — Short prefix skips prefilter:
- test_session_command_one_char_prefix_skips_prefilter: 1-char prefix
  bypasses prefilter, all sessions parsed
- test_session_command_three_char_prefix_skips_prefilter: 3-char prefix
  with no match shows error + Available list

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix vUSB collector: use isinstance() for USB device detection instead of string matching

The vUSB collector was using string matching on type names (e.g., 'VirtualUSB')
but pyVmomi returns fully qualified class names (e.g., 'vim.vm.device.VirtualUSB').
This caused the collector to never detect any USB devices, resulting in empty
vUSB sheets.

Fixed by:
- Replacing string type comparison with isinstance() checks
- Using proper vim.vm.device.VirtualUSB/Controller/XHCIController types
- Extracting short device names for display using split('.')[-1]

This is a critical fix that restores vUSB sheet functionality.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: narrow exception handler and improve ensure_aware docstring (#410)

- Narrow except Exception to except (OSError, RuntimeError) in observer
  cleanup path in cli.py to avoid swallowing unexpected errors
- Extend ensure_aware docstring with UTC-assumption warning in models.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: cover model-known + zero-output-tokens active session state (#408)

Add two tests to TestConfigModelReading that exercise the previously
untested fourth state of the active_metrics guard in build_session_summary:
model is resolved (non-None) but total_output_tokens == 0.

- test_active_session_model_known_zero_tokens_has_empty_metrics:
  model from tool.execution_complete, no assistant messages
- test_active_session_config_model_zero_tokens_empty_metrics:
  model from config.json, no assistant messages

Both assert model is set, model_metrics == {}, active_output_tokens == 0,
and is_active == True, documenting the deliberate design that a 0-token
entry is not created.

Closes #408

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
